title: Tunisian Beef Kidney Tagine
description: A rich and romantic Tunisian dish featuring beef kidney in a molten sauce, free from onions and garlic, making it suitable for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the beef kidney pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the lemon juice, paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth, dried apricots, and prunes, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the tagine or Dutch oven, and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the kidney is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Traditionally, the tagine is served with crusty bread or over couscous.
You can also serve it with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
